description: S97 REAL-UI tap upgrade of the notification-sound toggle (NOVEL, UNVALIDATED, nonBlocking): tap the real Settings notification-sound Switch (UiKeys.settingsNotificationSoundSwitch = 'settings_notification_sound_switch' @ lib/ui/settings/global_settings_section.dart:492) via the marionette tap action and prove notificationSound flips in l3_dump_state (true->false), then tap again to restore. THREE BLOCKERS make this nonBlocking and OWE live-validation + codex-review: (1) NO existing scenario uses the tap/enter_text actions — this is a novel, never-run pattern; (2) the marionette ext (ext.flutter.marionette.tap) vs working binding is unresolved (MCP_BINDING=skill lacks the marionette ext; MCP_BINDING=marionette can hang startup); (3) the runner has no scroll action and the notification-sound Switch sits below the settings-page fold (Global Settings section), so the tap may fail to hit a laid-out widget. The PROVEN data-half is l3_notification_sound_toggle.json (S86) — that hard gate drives l3_set_setting notificationSound and is what actually passes today. The proper executable form may be a flutter test WidgetTester gate (REAL_UI_GATES recipe), not this JSON. Soft precondition (starts true, the seeded default) catches a dirty fixture; ends restored to true so the fixture self-cleans ON SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION only (no `teardown` phase — a mid-run failure/interruption can leave the setting flipped; re-run or restore manually).
